1	# SCRATCH.md -- Comprehensive Codebase Reference
2	
3	## 1. Project Overview
4	
5	**What:** A real-time collaborative whiteboard. Multiple users share a canvas, draw shapes, see each other's cursors, and persist state across sessions.
6	
7	**Deployment context:** 2-5 people on a video call doing synchronous design reviews. Sessions run 30-90 minutes. Teams revisit canvases later. Concurrent same-object edits are common.
8	
9	**Origin:** Mechanize take-home interview assignment (3-hour solo block, followed by a defense call).
10	
11	**Tech Stack (locked):**
12	
13	| Layer       | Technology                                   |
14	|-------------|----------------------------------------------|
15	| Frontend    | React 19, TypeScript 5.6, Vite 6             |
16	| Backend     | FastAPI (Starlette), Python 3.12, Uvicorn    |
17	| Database    | PostgreSQL 16                                |
18	| DB driver   | asyncpg (async, `$1` param syntax)           |
19	| Auth        | JWT (HS256) via PyJWT, bcrypt password hashes |
20	| Transport   | Single WebSocket per user per canvas + HTTP for auth/CRUD |
21	| Containers  | Docker Compose (db, server, client)          |
22	
23	---
24	
25	## 2. Architecture Diagram
26	
27	```
28	+---------------------------+       +----------------------------+
29	|        Browser (React)    |       |        Browser (React)     |
30	|                           |       |                            |
31	|  Auth -> localStorage     |       |  Auth -> localStorage      |
32	|  CanvasPage -> <canvas>   |       |  CanvasPage -> <canvas>    |
33	|                           |       |                            |
34	|  HTTP: /api/auth/*        |       |  HTTP: /api/auth/*         |
35	|  HTTP: /api/canvases/*    |       |  HTTP: /api/canvases/*     |
36	|  WS:   /ws?canvasId&token |       |  WS:   /ws?canvasId&token  |
37	+----------+-------+--------+       +--------+--------+----------+
38	           |       |                          |        |
39	     HTTP  |       | WS                 HTTP  |        | WS
40	           |       |                          |        |
41	+----------v-------v--------------------------v--------v----------+
42	|                    FastAPI Server (:3001)                        |
43	|                                                                 |
44	|  main.py          -- lifespan, CORS, error handler, route wiring|
45	|  auth.py          -- POST /signup, /login, GET /me              |
46	|  canvas.py        -- GET/POST /canvases, GET /:id, POST /invite |
47	|  ws.py            -- WS /ws: auth, init, ops, cursors, presence |
48	|  deps.py          -- get_current_user (JWT decode from header)  |
49	|  db.py            -- asyncpg pool singleton                     |
50	|  types.py         -- shared TypedDicts (mirror of types.ts)     |
51	|                                                                 |
52	|  In-memory state (ws.py module globals):                        |
53	|    presence{}     -- canvas_id -> user_id -> {ws, color, ...}   |
54	|    seq_counters{} -- canvas_id -> int (monotonic)               |
55	|    canvas_locks{} -- canvas_id -> asyncio.Lock                  |
56	+--------------------------+--------------------------------------+
57	                           |
58	                           | asyncpg (TCP :5432)
59	                           |
60	            +--------------v--------------+
61	            |     PostgreSQL 16 (:5432)   |
62	            |                             |
63	            |  users                      |
64	            |  canvases                   |
65	            |  canvas_members             |
66	            |  shapes                     |
67	            +-----------------------------+
68	
69	Data flow summary:
70	  HTTP: auth + canvas CRUD (JSON over fetch)
71	  WS:   ops + cursors + presence + ping/pong heartbeat (JSON over single socket per user per canvas)
72	  DB:   shapes are mutated per-op (no op log); presence is memory-only
73	  Reconnection: client auto-reconnects with exponential backoff; server sends fresh init on reconnect
74	```
75	
76	---
77	
78	## 3. File Map
79	
80	### Root
81	
82	| File | Purpose |
83	|------|---------|
84	| `BUILD_CONTRACT.md` | Frozen contract: schemas, protocols, ownership map, dispatch prompts |
85	| `CLAUDE.md` | AI agent instructions: stack, conventions, gotchas, CVEs |
86	| `AGENTS.md` | Multi-agent ownership boundaries, handoff format, review checklist |
87	| `README.md` | Take-home assignment instructions and evaluation criteria |
88	| `SETUP.md` | How to run (Docker Compose and local dev) |
89	| `docker-compose.yml` | Three services: db (PG 16), server (FastAPI), client (Vite) |
90	| `package.json` | Monorepo root; npm workspaces pointing to `client/` |
91	| `.gitignore` | Excludes node_modules, dist, .env, __pycache__, .venv |
92	
93	### Server
94	
95	| File | Purpose |
96	|------|---------|
97	| `server/schema.sql` | DDL: users, canvases, canvas_members, shapes tables + indexes |
98	| `server/pyproject.toml` | Python project: fastapi, uvicorn, asyncpg, pyjwt, bcrypt |
99	| `server/Dockerfile` | Multi-stage build: compile C extensions in builder, slim runtime |
100	| `server/app/__init__.py` | Empty package marker |
101	| `server/app/main.py` | FastAPI app: lifespan (pool init/close), CORS, error handler, route wiring, `/health` |
102	| `server/app/db.py` | asyncpg pool singleton (`get_pool()`, `close_pool()`) |
103	| `server/app/deps.py` | `get_current_user()`: JWT decode from Authorization header, returns `AuthUser` |

105	| `server/app/auth.py` | `auth_router`: POST /signup, POST /login, GET /me |
106	| `server/app/canvas.py` | `canvas_router`: GET /, POST /, GET /{id}, POST /{id}/invite |
107	| `server/app/ws.py` | `websocket_endpoint()`: auth, init, op persist+broadcast, cursor relay, presence, ping/pong heartbeat (30s interval, 10s pong timeout) |
108	
109	### Client
110	
111	| File | Purpose |
112	|------|---------|
113	| `client/package.json` | React 19, Vite 6, TypeScript 5.6 dependencies |
114	| `client/tsconfig.json` | Strict mode, ES2022 target, react-jsx, bundler resolution |
115	| `client/vite.config.ts` | Dev proxy: `/api` -> `:3001`, `/ws` -> `ws://:3001` |
116	| `client/index.html` | SPA shell: `<div id="root">`, loads `/src/main.tsx` |
117	| `client/Dockerfile` | Node 22 Alpine, `npm install`, `npm run dev` |
118	| `client/src/types.ts` | CANONICAL shared contract: Shape, Operation, all message types |
119	| `client/src/main.tsx` | React 19 entry: StrictMode + createRoot |
120	| `client/src/App.tsx` | Root component: client-side routing via `Page` discriminated union |
121	| `client/src/api.ts` | HTTP client: typed fetch wrappers for all API endpoints |
122	| `client/src/authStore.ts` | localStorage-based token/user persistence |
123	| `client/src/operations.ts` | `applyOp()`: applies Operation to Shape[]; `reverseOp()`: builds undo inverse |
124	| `client/src/canvasRenderer.ts` | Canvas 2D rendering: dot-grid background, z-order rendering (selected shape drawn last), shapes, selection box, handles, cursors with opacity/lerp support, hit testing |
125	| `client/src/components/Auth.tsx` | Refined card-based login/signup UI with logo, subheadings, divider, styled inputs |
126	| `client/src/components/CanvasList.tsx` | Grid card layout for canvases with preview thumbnails, hover lift, empty state illustration |
127	| `client/src/components/CanvasPage.tsx` | Main whiteboard: WS connection with auto-reconnection (exponential backoff + jitter, manual retry), cursor lerp animation loop, resize normalization via DPR, input focus guard on keyboard shortcuts, ping/pong client handler |
128	| `client/src/components/Toolbar.tsx` | Floating bottom toolbar (dark, centered) with inline SVG tool icons, 8-color PALETTE swatches for fill/stroke, custom color picker, undo/redo SVG icons, separated back button (top-left) |
129	| `client/src/components/InvitePanel.tsx` | Compact avatar row (pill-shaped, top-right) with colored initial circles, dashed "+" invite button, dropdown form for username/email invite |
130	
131	---
132	
133	## 4. Data Flow
134	
135	### 4a. Auth Flow
136	
137	```
138	User fills signup form
139	  -> Auth.tsx handleSubmit()
140	    -> api.ts signup({ username, email, password })
141	      -> POST /api/auth/signup (JSON body)
142	        -> auth.py: hash password with bcrypt, INSERT INTO users
143	        -> Return { token: JWT, user: AuthUser }
144	      <- api.ts returns AuthResponse
145	    -> authStore.ts storeAuth(token, user) -- saves to localStorage
146	    -> App.tsx handleAuth() -- setPage({ kind: "canvases" })
147	
148	Subsequent requests:
149	  -> api.ts authHeaders() reads token from localStorage
150	  -> Sends Authorization: Bearer <token>
151	  -> deps.py get_current_user() decodes JWT, returns AuthUser
152	  -> If expired/invalid: 401 -> ApiError -> redirect to login
153	
154	JWT payload: { sub: userId, username, email, exp: now + 24h }
155	Signed with JWT_SECRET (HS256)
156	```
157	
158	### 4b. Canvas CRUD Flow
159	
160	```
161	CanvasList mounts
162	  -> useEffect calls api.getCanvases()
163	    -> GET /api/canvases (authed)
164	      -> canvas.py: SELECT canvases JOIN canvas_members WHERE user_id = $1
165	      -> Returns CanvasSummary[]
166	
167	User creates canvas:
168	  -> handleCreate() -> api.createCanvas({ name })
169	    -> POST /api/canvases (authed)
170	      -> canvas.py: INSERT canvases + INSERT canvas_members (transaction)
171	      -> Returns CanvasSummary
172	
173	User clicks canvas:
174	  -> onSelectCanvas(canvasId) -> App sets page to { kind: "canvas", canvasId }
175	  -> CanvasPage mounts -> loads detail + connects WS (see 4c)
176	
177	User invites:
178	  -> InvitePanel handleInvite()
179	    -> api.inviteToCanvas(canvasId, { identifier })
180	      -> POST /api/canvases/{id}/invite
181	        -> canvas.py: look up user by username, then email
182	        -> INSERT canvas_members ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
183	        -> Returns { ok: true }
184	```
185	
186	### 4c. Drawing Flow (shape creation)
187	
188	```
189	User selects Rectangle tool, mousedown on canvas
190	  -> handleMouseDown(): creates previewShape with crypto.randomUUID()
191	  -> dragModeRef = { kind: "draw", startX, startY, previewShape }
192	
193	User drags mouse
194	  -> handleMouseMove(): updates previewShape dimensions
195	  -> requestRender() -> renderScene() draws preview on canvas
196	
197	User releases mouse
198	  -> handleMouseUp():
199	    -> if shape has size > 2px:
200	      -> Build forward = { kind: "add", shape }
201	      -> Build reverse = { kind: "delete", shapeId }
202	      -> doAction(forward, reverse):
203	        -> Push { forward, reverse } onto undoStack, clear redoStack
204	        -> sendOp(forward):
205	          -> Apply optimistically: shapesRef.current = applyOp(shapes, op)
206	          -> Generate opId = crypto.randomUUID()
207	          -> Add opId to pendingOps
208	          -> ws.send({ type: "op", op, opId })
209	
210	Server receives op (ws.py):
211	  -> Acquire canvas_locks[canvasId]
212	  -> _persist_add(): INSERT INTO shapes ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
213	  -> If inserted (modified=True):
214	    -> Increment seq_counters[canvasId]
215	    -> Release lock
216	    -> _broadcast_all(): send { type: "op", op, userId, seq, opId } to ALL clients
217	
218	Sender receives echo:
219	  -> ws.onmessage "op":
220	    -> pendingOps.has(opId) = true -> delete it
221	    -> op.kind === "add" -> skip (already applied optimistically)
222	
223	Other client receives op:
224	  -> pendingOps.has(opId) = false
225	    -> applyOp(shapes, op) -> shape added to their shapesRef
226	    -> requestRender() -> shape appears on their canvas
227	```
228	
229	### 4d. Undo/Redo Flow
230	
231	```
232	Undo (Ctrl+Z):
233	  -> undo():
234	    -> Pop last entry from undoStack
235	    -> Push to redoStack
236	    -> sendOp(entry.reverse):
237	      -> Apply reverse op optimistically
238	      -> Send as normal op over WS
239	  -> Server persists the reverse op (e.g., DELETE for an add's undo)
240	  -> Server broadcasts to all clients
241	
242	Redo (Ctrl+Shift+Z):
243	  -> redo():
244	    -> Pop last entry from redoStack
245	    -> Push to undoStack
246	    -> sendOp(entry.forward)
247	  -> Same persist + broadcast cycle
248	
249	Reverse op construction (operations.ts reverseOp()):
250	  - add -> delete (shapeId)
251	  - delete -> add (snapshot of shape before deletion)
252	  - update -> update (only changed fields' old values)
253	  - Returns null if shape not found (already deleted by another user)
254	
255	Key: server sees undo/redo as normal ops. No special undo protocol.
256	```
257	
258	### 4e. Cursor Presence Flow
259	
260	```
261	User moves mouse on canvas
262	  -> handleMouseMove():
263	    -> Throttle check: Date.now() - lastCursorSendRef >= 30ms
264	    -> sendWs({ type: "cursor", x, y })
265	
266	Server receives cursor (ws.py):
267	  -> _broadcast() to all clients EXCEPT sender:
268	    -> { type: "cursor", userId, username, x, y }
269	
270	Other client receives cursor:
271	  -> ws.onmessage "cursor":
272	    -> cursorTargetsRef.current.set(userId, { x, y, timestamp })
273	    -> cursorsRef.current entry created (first time) or metadata updated
274	    -> Cursor lerp rAF loop interpolates displayed position toward target
275	    -> canvasRenderer.ts drawCursor(): arrow + username label, respects opacity
276	
277	User connects:
278	  -> Server adds to presence[canvasId][userId]
279	  -> Assigns color from CURSOR_PALETTE (round-robin by current count)
280	  -> Sends "init" with all current users
281	  -> Broadcasts "join" to others
282	
283	User disconnects:
284	  -> Server removes from presence
285	  -> Broadcasts "leave" to remaining clients
286	  -> Client removes cursor from map on "leave" message
287	```
288	
289	---
290	
291	## 5. Database Schema
292	
293	```sql
294	-- pgcrypto enables gen_random_uuid()
295	CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pgcrypto;
296	
297	CREATE TABLE users (
298	    id            UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
299	    username      TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
300	    email         TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
301	    password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
302	    created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
303	);
304	
305	CREATE TABLE canvases (
306	    id         UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
307	    name       TEXT NOT NULL,
308	    owner_id   UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
309	    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
310	);
311	
312	CREATE TABLE canvas_members (
313	    canvas_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES canvases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
314	    user_id   UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
315	    PRIMARY KEY (canvas_id, user_id)
316	);
317	
318	CREATE TABLE shapes (
319	    id           UUID PRIMARY KEY,              -- client-generated, no default
320	    canvas_id    UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES canvases(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
321	    type         TEXT NOT NULL,                  -- rectangle|ellipse|line|text
322	    x            DOUBLE PRECISION NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
323	    y            DOUBLE PRECISION NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
324	    width        DOUBLE PRECISION NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
325	    height       DOUBLE PRECISION NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
326	    fill         TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
327	    stroke       TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '#000000',
328	    stroke_width DOUBLE PRECISION NOT NULL DEFAULT 2,
329	    text         TEXT,                           -- nullable, for text shapes
330	    font_size    DOUBLE PRECISION                -- nullable, for text shapes
331	);
332	
333	CREATE INDEX idx_shapes_canvas ON shapes(canvas_id);
334	CREATE INDEX idx_canvas_members_user ON canvas_members(user_id);
335	```
336	
337	**Relationships:**
338	- `canvases.owner_id` -> `users.id` (no cascade -- owner deletion not handled)
339	- `canvas_members` is a join table: composite PK `(canvas_id, user_id)`, both FK with CASCADE
340	- `shapes.canvas_id` -> `canvases.id` ON DELETE CASCADE
341	
342	**Column naming:**
343	- DB uses `snake_case`: `stroke_width`, `font_size`, `owner_id`, `created_at`
344	- Wire format uses `camelCase`: `strokeWidth`, `fontSize`, `ownerId`, `createdAt`
345	- Mapping defined in `types.py`: `SHAPE_DB_TO_WIRE` / `SHAPE_WIRE_TO_DB`
346	
347	---
348	
349	## 6. WebSocket Protocol
350	
351	### Connection
352	
353	```
354	WS /ws?canvasId=<uuid>&token=<jwt>
355	```
356	
357	Server validates JWT and canvas membership BEFORE `ws.accept()`.
358	- Close `4001`: auth failure (bad/missing token, bad canvasId)
359	- Close `4003`: not a canvas member
360	
361	### Client -> Server
362	
363	| Type | Fields | Description |
364	|------|--------|-------------|
365	| `op` | `type: "op"`, `op: Operation`, `opId: string` | Board operation (add/update/delete) with client-generated UUID |
366	| `cursor` | `type: "cursor"`, `x: number`, `y: number` | Cursor position in canvas coordinates |
367	| `pong` | `type: "pong"` | Reply to server ping (heartbeat) |
368	
369	`Operation` is one of:
370	- `{ kind: "add", shape: Shape }` -- full Shape object with client-generated id
371	- `{ kind: "update", shapeId: string, props: ShapePatch }` -- partial field update
372	- `{ kind: "delete", shapeId: string }` -- remove by id
373	
374	### Server -> Client
375	
376	| Type | Fields | Description |
377	|------|--------|-------------|
378	| `init` | `type`, `shapes: Shape[]`, `users: PresenceUser[]`, `seq: number` | Sent on connect. Full current state. |
379	| `op` | `type`, `op: Operation`, `userId: string`, `seq: number`, `opId: string` | Persisted op echoed to ALL clients (including sender) |
380	| `cursor` | `type`, `userId: string`, `username: string`, `x: number`, `y: number` | Cursor position from another user (never sent to self) |
381	| `join` | `type`, `user: PresenceUser` | New user connected (sent to others) |
382	| `leave` | `type`, `userId: string` | User disconnected (sent to others) |
383	| `ping` | `type: "ping"` | Server heartbeat probe (every 30s). Client must reply with `pong` within 10s or connection is closed. |
384	| `error` | `type`, `message: string` | Error message |
385	
386	`PresenceUser`: `{ userId, username, color }`
387	
388	### Server Op Processing (steps from contract)
389	
390	1. Receive `{ type: "op", op, opId }`
391	2. Acquire per-canvas `asyncio.Lock`
392	3. Persist to DB (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE)
393	4. If no-op (row not modified): release lock, stop. No broadcast, no seq.
394	5. Increment `seq_counters[canvasId]`
395	6. Release lock
396	7. Broadcast `{ type: "op", op, userId, seq, opId }` to all clients
397	
398	---
399	
400	## 7. Key Design Decisions
401	
402	### 7a. Server-Authoritative LWW with Seq Numbers
403	
404	The server is the single source of truth. There is no OT or CRDT. Every successful mutation gets a per-canvas monotonic `seq` number. Clients track `seqRef` but currently don't use it for ordering -- they apply ops as they arrive. `seq` primarily exists to detect how far behind a client is and to enable future reconnection logic.
405	
406	Conflict resolution is **last-write-wins at field level by server arrival order**. If User A sets `fill: red` and User B sets `fill: blue`, whichever the server processes second wins. Because `ShapePatch` is partial, different-field edits (e.g., A changes fill, B changes x) never conflict.
407	
408	### 7b. Optimistic Updates with opId Reconciliation
409	
410	```typescript
411	// CanvasPage.tsx sendOp():
412	const opId = crypto.randomUUID();
413	shapesRef.current = applyOp(shapesRef.current, op);  // optimistic
414	pendingOpsRef.current.add(opId);
415	ws.send({ type: "op", op, opId });
416	```
417	
418	When the server echo comes back:
419	
420	```typescript
421	// ws.onmessage "op":
422	const isOwnEcho = pendingOpsRef.current.has(msg.opId);
423	if (isOwnEcho) pendingOpsRef.current.delete(msg.opId);
424	
425	if (isOwnEcho && msg.op.kind === "add") {
426	  // Skip -- shape already in array from optimistic add
427	} else {
428	  shapesRef.current = applyOp(shapesRef.current, msg.op);
429	  // Re-applies update/delete even for own ops
430	}
431	```
432	
433	**Why update/delete echoes are re-applied for own ops:** Under concurrent edits, the optimistic local state may have been overwritten by a remote op that arrived between the local apply and the server echo. Re-applying the server-authoritative version ensures convergence. For `add`, re-applying would create a duplicate, so it's skipped.
434	
435	If the server ignores a no-op (e.g., deleting an already-deleted shape), the pending opId is never removed. The optimistic state diverges until the next `init` on reconnect corrects it. This is an acceptable tradeoff given the deployment context.
436	
437	### 7c. Per-Canvas asyncio.Lock for Op Serialization
438	
439	```python
440	# ws.py
441	canvas_locks: dict[str, asyncio.Lock] = defaultdict(asyncio.Lock)
442	
443	async with lock:
444	    async with pool.acquire(timeout=5) as conn:
445	        modified = await _persist_add(conn, canvas_id, shape)
446	    if modified:
447	        seq_counters[canvas_id] += 1
448	        seq = seq_counters[canvas_id]
449	# Lock released BEFORE broadcast
450	```
451	
452	One lock per canvas ensures ops for the same canvas are serialized -- no interleaved reads/writes to shapes, no seq races. Different canvases are fully parallel. The lock is released before the broadcast to avoid holding it during potentially slow network I/O.
453	
454	### 7d. Client-Side Undo/Redo with Field-Level Reverse Ops
455	
456	```typescript
457	// operations.ts reverseOp():
458	case "update": {
459	  const reverseProps: ShapePatch = {};
460	  for (const key of Object.keys(op.props)) {
461	    reverseProps[key] = shape[key];  // capture old value of ONLY changed fields
462	  }
463	  return { kind: "update", shapeId, props: reverseProps };
464	}
465	```
466	
467	Undo/redo are purely client-side stacks. The server never sees "undo" as a concept -- it receives the reverse operation as a normal op. This means:
468	
469	- Different-field concurrent edits survive undo (field-level capture, not snapshot)
470	- Undo of "add" sends "delete" -- even if others modified the shape
471	- Undo of "delete" sends "add" with a pre-delete snapshot -- later concurrent changes are lost
472	- If the target shape no longer exists, the server ignores the op (no-op)
473	- `reverseOp()` returns `null` if the shape is already gone, and the caller skips pushing to the undo stack
474	
475	### 7e. Refs vs useState Split in React
476	
477	CanvasPage uses two categories of state:
478	
479	**Refs** (high-frequency, no re-render needed):
480	- `shapesRef` -- shapes array (updated 60fps during drag, on every WS op)
481	- `cursorsRef` -- remote cursor displayed positions (interpolated by lerp loop)
482	- `cursorTargetsRef` -- remote cursor target positions (set on each WS cursor message)
483	- `dragModeRef` -- current drag interaction state
484	- `selectedIdRef` -- currently selected shape
485	- `pendingOpsRef` -- Set of opIds awaiting server echo
486	- `undoStackRef` / `redoStackRef` -- undo stacks
487	- `seqRef` -- latest server seq
488	- `wsRef` -- WebSocket instance
489	- `lastCursorSendRef` -- throttle timestamp
490	- `reconnectAttemptRef` / `reconnectTimerRef` / `shouldReconnectRef` / `connectWsFnRef` -- reconnection state
491	- `lerpAnimFrameRef` -- rAF handle for cursor lerp loop
492	
493	**useState** (triggers re-renders for UI):
494	- `currentTool`, `fillColor`, `strokeColor` -- toolbar state
495	- `undoCount`, `redoCount` -- enables/disables undo/redo buttons
496	- `onlineUsers` -- user list in invite panel
497	- `wsConnected`, `wsStatus`, `reconnectFailed` -- connection indicator (three-state: connected/reconnecting/disconnected)
498	- `error` -- error banner
499	
500	**Why:** React 19 batches setState calls, and rapid WS messages would either cause stale closures or dropped updates if shapes were in useState. By keeping shapes in a ref and using `requestAnimationFrame` for rendering, the canvas bypasses React's reconciliation entirely. The canvas 2D context is imperatively drawn, not declaratively rendered.
501	
502	### 7f. Per-Connection Send Locks (Frame Interleaving Prevention)
503	
504	```python
505	# ws.py
506	async def _safe_send(ws, data, send_lock):
507	    try:
508	        async with send_lock:
509	            await ws.send_text(data)
510	    except Exception:
511	        pass  # connection closed; cleanup happens in finally block
512	
513	# Each connection gets its own lock:
514	send_lock = asyncio.Lock()
515	canvas_presence[user_id] = { "ws": ws, "send_lock": send_lock, ... }
516	```
517	
518	Starlette does NOT serialize concurrent `send_text()` calls on the same WebSocket. If two async tasks broadcast simultaneously, their frames can interleave and corrupt messages. The per-connection `asyncio.Lock` serializes all sends to a given client. The lock is per-connection, not per-canvas, so sends to different clients remain parallel.
519	
520	### 7g. Column Whitelisting for SQL Injection Prevention
521	
522	```python
523	# ws.py
524	_ALLOWED_SHAPE_COLS = frozenset({
525	    "id", "type", "x", "y", "width", "height",
526	    "fill", "stroke", "stroke_width", "text", "font_size",
527	})
528	
529	def _shape_to_columns(shape: dict):
530	    for wire_key, value in shape.items():
531	        db_key = SHAPE_WIRE_TO_DB.get(wire_key, wire_key)
532	        if db_key not in _ALLOWED_SHAPE_COLS:
533	            continue  # silently drop unknown keys
534	```
535	
536	Because shape data comes from the WebSocket (untrusted JSON), and column names are interpolated into SQL via f-strings (`INSERT INTO shapes ({col_names})`), a malicious client could inject SQL through crafted key names. The whitelist ensures only known column names are ever placed into queries. Values always go through asyncpg's parameterized `$N` placeholders.
537	
538	The same pattern applies to `_ALLOWED_UPDATE_COLS` (which additionally excludes `id` and `type` since those are immutable).
539	
540	### 7h. Floating Bottom Toolbar with SVG Icons and Color Palette
541	
542	The toolbar is a dark (`#2c2c2c`) floating bar, fixed at bottom-center with `position: fixed`. Tool buttons use inline SVG path icons (not icon fonts or images). Fill and stroke each expose an 8-color `PALETTE` as swatch buttons plus a native `<input type="color">` picker behind a "+" affordance. The back button is separated from the bar and placed top-left. Groups (tools, colors, undo/redo) are visually separated by 1px dividers.
543	
544	### 7i. Dot-Grid Background and Z-Order Rendering
545	
546	`canvasRenderer.ts` draws a dot grid (1px dots at 20px spacing, color `#d4d5db`) over the `#f8f9fb` background on every frame. Shapes are drawn in array order (creation order), except the currently selected shape is skipped on the first pass and drawn last -- this gives the selected shape a visual "lift" above all others without mutating the shapes array. Preview shapes (during draw-drag) render after the selected shape.
547	
548	### 7j. WebSocket Reconnection with Exponential Backoff
549	
550	```typescript
551	// CanvasPage.tsx
552	const MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS = 10;
553	const BACKOFF_DELAYS = [1000, 2000, 4000, 8000, 16000, 30000]; // cap at 30s
554	// Jitter: delay * (0.5 + Math.random() * 0.5)
555	```
556	
557	On unexpected WS close, the client retries up to 10 times with capped exponential backoff plus random jitter. Auth failures (close codes `4001`, `4003`) skip reconnection entirely. After max attempts, a red banner appears: "Connection lost. Click to retry." which resets the counter. On successful reconnect, the server sends a fresh `init` message, which resets shapes, presence, undo/redo stacks, and pending ops. Connection status is displayed as a colored pill (green/yellow/red) in the bottom-left corner with three states: `connected`, `reconnecting`, `disconnected`.
558	
559	### 7k. Server-Side Ping/Pong Heartbeat
560	
561	```python
562	# ws.py
563	PING_INTERVAL = 30  # seconds between pings
564	PONG_TIMEOUT = 10   # seconds to wait for pong reply
565	```
566	
567	The server runs a `_heartbeat_loop` asyncio task per connection. Every 30 seconds it sends `{"type": "ping"}` over the WebSocket. If no `{"type": "pong"}` is received within 10 seconds, the server closes the connection with code `1000` ("pong timeout"). The heartbeat task is cancelled via a `cancel_event` in the connection's `finally` block. The client handles `ping` messages before casting to the typed `ServerMessage` union (since `ping` is not in the union type).
568	
569	### 7l. Cursor Lerp Interpolation and Opacity Fade
570	
571	```typescript
572	// CanvasPage.tsx cursor lerp loop
573	const LERP_FACTOR = 0.15;
574	const FADE_AFTER_MS = 5000;
575	const FADED_OPACITY = 0.3;
576	```
577	
578	Remote cursor positions are stored as *targets* in a separate `cursorTargetsRef` map. A dedicated `requestAnimationFrame` loop runs continuously, interpolating each cursor's displayed position toward its target at a factor of 0.15 per frame. Cursors snap to the target when within 0.1px. After 5 seconds without a new cursor message, the cursor's opacity fades to 0.3 (rendered via `ctx.globalAlpha` in `drawCursor()`). The `CursorInfo` type includes an optional `opacity` field for this.
579	
580	---
581	
582	## 8. Known Issues / Integration Notes
583	
584	### 8a. Error Envelope Handler in main.py
585	
586	```python
587	@app.exception_handler(HTTPException)
588	async def http_exception_handler(request, exc):
589	    detail = exc.detail if isinstance(exc.detail, str) else "error"
590	    return JSONResponse(status_code=exc.status_code, content={"error": detail})
591	```
592	
593	This overrides FastAPI's default exception handler so ALL HTTPExceptions return `{"error": "<code>"}` instead of `{"detail": "<message>"}`. This is critical for the client's `ApiError` class, which reads `err.error` from the response body. Without this handler, auth failures would return `{"detail": "unauthorized"}` and the client would see `error: "unknown"`.
594	
595	Note that `auth.py` and `canvas.py` return `JSONResponse` directly for error cases (not raising HTTPException), so this handler only catches errors from `deps.py get_current_user()` and any unhandled FastAPI validation errors.
596	
597	### 8b. UUID String-to-Object Conversion in ws.py
598	
599	asyncpg requires `uuid.UUID` objects for UUID columns, not strings. The WebSocket handler receives shape IDs as strings from JSON and must convert them:
600	
601	```python
602	if db_key == "id" and isinstance(value, str):
603	    value = _uuid.UUID(value)
604	```
605	
606	This conversion happens in `_shape_to_columns()` for inserts, and explicitly in `_persist_update()` and `_persist_delete()` for WHERE clauses. Canvas ID and user ID are similarly converted at the top of `websocket_endpoint()`. Failure to convert would cause asyncpg `DataError`.
607	
608	### 8c. StrictMode Double-Mount Guard
609	
610	```typescript
611	// CanvasPage.tsx
612	const mountedRef = useRef(false);
613	
614	useEffect(() => {
615	    if (mountedRef.current) return;  // skip second mount in StrictMode
616	    mountedRef.current = true;
617	    // ... connect WS, load canvas ...
618	    return () => {
619	        mountedRef.current = false;
620	        ws.close();
621	    };
622	}, [canvasId]);
623	```
624	
625	React 19 StrictMode in development double-fires effects (mount -> unmount -> remount). Without this guard, two WebSocket connections would open simultaneously, causing duplicate messages, presence entries, and duplicate reconnection timers. The `mountedRef` flag ensures only the first mount actually connects. The cleanup function sets `mountedRef = false`, clears `shouldReconnectRef`, cancels any pending reconnect timer, and closes the WebSocket.
626	
627	### 8d. HTTP/WS Init Race Condition Fix
628	
629	```typescript
630	// CanvasPage.tsx
631	const wsInitReceivedRef = useRef(false);
632	
633	// HTTP load (fallback):
634	getCanvasDetail(canvasId).then((detail) => {
635	    if (!wsInitReceivedRef.current) {
636	        shapesRef.current = detail.shapes;
637	    }
638	});
639	
640	// WS init (authoritative):
641	case "init":
642	    wsInitReceivedRef.current = true;
643	    shapesRef.current = msg.shapes;
644	```
645	
646	Both an HTTP GET and a WebSocket connection are initiated on mount. The HTTP response might arrive first with stale data, then the WS `init` arrives with the correct current state (including any ops that occurred between the HTTP response and WS connection). The `wsInitReceivedRef` flag ensures that if the WS init has already arrived, the HTTP response doesn't overwrite it with potentially stale data.
647	
648	### 8e. Input Focus Guard on Keyboard Shortcuts
649	
650	```typescript
651	// CanvasPage.tsx handleKeyDown():
652	const tag = (e.target as HTMLElement)?.tagName;
653	if (tag === "INPUT" || tag === "TEXTAREA" || (e.target as HTMLElement)?.isContentEditable) return;
654	```
655	
656	Keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+Z, Ctrl+Shift+Z, Delete/Backspace) are skipped when focus is on an input, textarea, or contentEditable element. This prevents undo/delete from firing while the user types in the invite panel or canvas name input.
657	
658	### 8f. What's NOT Implemented
659	
660	| Feature | Status |
661	|---------|--------|
662	| **WebSocket reconnection** | Implemented. Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff (up to 10 attempts, max 30s delay with jitter). Manual retry button after exhaustion. See 7j. |
663	| **Ping/pong heartbeat** | Implemented. Server pings every 30s, closes connection if no pong within 10s. See 7k. |
664	| **Cursor interpolation (lerp)** | Implemented. rAF loop interpolates at factor 0.15 per frame; opacity fades to 0.3 after 5s idle. See 7l. |
665	| **Reconnect state recovery** | No incremental sync. Reconnect triggers a fresh `init` from the server, resetting shapes, presence, undo/redo stacks, and pending ops. |
666	| **Rate limiting** | No connection or message rate limits on WS or HTTP. |
667	| **Input validation** | Minimal. WS messages with missing fields are silently ignored, but no schema validation. |
668	| **Text editing in-place** | Text creation uses `window.prompt()`. No in-canvas text editing overlay. |
669	| **Z-order manipulation** | Shapes are z-ordered by creation time (array index). Selected shape is drawn last for visual lift but array order is not mutated. No bring-to-front/send-to-back user action. |
670	| **Multi-select** | Only single shape selection. No box select or Shift+click. |
671	| **Pending ops divergence recovery** | If server ignores an op (no-op), the pendingOps set grows unboundedly until reconnect. |
672	| **seq counter persistence** | seq resets on server restart. Clients self-correct on reconnect via fresh `init`. |
673	| **Connection pool tuning for WS** | Pool is min=2, max=20. Each WS op holds a connection briefly. Could exhaust under load. |
674	| **Structured logging** | No logging anywhere in the backend. Errors are silently caught in WS handler. |
675	
676	---
677	
678	## 9. How to Run
679	
680	### Docker Compose (all services)
681	
682	```bash
683	docker compose up --build
684	```
685	
686	This starts:
687	- PostgreSQL on `:5432` (schema.sql runs on first boot via initdb.d mount)
688	- FastAPI server on `:3001` (waits for PG health check)
689	- Vite dev server on `:5173`
690	
691	Open `http://localhost:5173`.
692	
693	**Reset database** (if schema.sql changes):
694	```bash
695	docker compose down -v   # removes pgdata volume
696	docker compose up --build
697	```
698	
699	### Local Dev (DB in Docker, app native)
700	
701	```bash
702	# Terminal 1: Start just the database
703	docker compose up db
704	
705	# Terminal 2: Initialize schema and start backend
706	psql postgres://whiteboard:whiteboard@localhost:5432/whiteboard -f server/schema.sql
707	cd server
708	pip install -e .
709	uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 3001
710	
711	# Terminal 3: Start frontend
712	cd client
713	npm install
714	npm run dev
715	```
716	
717	### Key Environment Variables
718	
719	| Variable | Used By | Default | Required |
720	|----------|---------|---------|----------|
721	| `DATABASE_URL` | server | `postgres://whiteboard:whiteboard@localhost:5432/whiteboard` | No |
722	| `JWT_SECRET` | server | `dev-secret-do-not-use-in-prod` | Yes (has fallback) |
723	| `VITE_WS_URL` | client (Docker only) | `ws://localhost:3001` | No |
724	| `VITE_API_URL` | client (Docker only) | `http://localhost:3001` | No |
725	
726	In local dev, the Vite proxy handles routing `/api` and `/ws` to `:3001`, so `VITE_*` vars are unnecessary. The client code constructs the WS URL from `window.location`:
727	
728	```typescript
729	const protocol = window.location.protocol === "https:" ? "wss:" : "ws:";
730	const host = window.location.host;
731	const wsUrl = `${protocol}//${host}/ws?canvasId=${canvasId}&token=${token}`;
732	```
733	
734	### Linting / Type Checking
735	
736	```bash
737	cd server && ruff check .        # Python linting
738	cd client && npx tsc --noEmit    # TypeScript type checking
739	```

1	"""Canvas CRUD and invite endpoints.
2	
3	Exports ``canvas_router`` to be mounted at ``/api/canvases``.
4	"""
5	
6	from __future__ import annotations
7	
8	import uuid as _uuid
9	
10	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends
11	from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
12	from pydantic import BaseModel
13	
14	from app.db import get_pool
15	from app.deps import get_current_user
16	from app.types import AuthUser, CanvasDetail, CanvasMember, CanvasSummary
17	
18	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
19	# Request body models (Pydantic for validation, matching contract TypedDicts)
20	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
21	
22	
23	class CreateCanvasBody(BaseModel):
24	    name: str
25	
26	
27	class InviteBody(BaseModel):
28	    identifier: str
29	
30	
31	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
32	# Router
33	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
34	
35	canvas_router = APIRouter()
36	
37	
38	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
39	# GET / — list canvases where the authenticated user is a member
40	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
41	
42	
43	@canvas_router.get("")
44	async def list_canvases(
45	    user: AuthUser = Depends(get_current_user),
46	) -> list[CanvasSummary]:
47	    pool = await get_pool()
48	    uid = _uuid.UUID(user["id"])
49	    async with pool.acquire() as conn:
50	        rows = await conn.fetch(
51	            """
52	            SELECT c.id, c.name, c.owner_id, c.created_at
53	            FROM canvases c
54	            JOIN canvas_members cm ON cm.canvas_id = c.id
55	            WHERE cm.user_id = $1
56	            ORDER BY c.created_at DESC
57	            """,
58	            uid,
59	        )
60	    return [
61	        CanvasSummary(
62	            id=str(row["id"]),
63	            name=row["name"],
64	            ownerId=str(row["owner_id"]),
65	            createdAt=row["created_at"].isoformat(),
66	        )
67	        for row in rows
68	    ]
69	
70	
71	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
72	# POST / — create a canvas and auto-add the creator as a member
73	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
74	
75	
76	@canvas_router.post("")
77	async def create_canvas(
78	    body: CreateCanvasBody,
79	    user: AuthUser = Depends(get_current_user),
80	) -> CanvasSummary:
81	    pool = await get_pool()
82	    uid = _uuid.UUID(user["id"])
83	    async with pool.acquire() as conn:
84	        async with conn.transaction():
85	            row = await conn.fetchrow(
86	                """
87	                INSERT INTO canvases (name, owner_id)
88	                VALUES ($1, $2)
89	                RETURNING id, name, owner_id, created_at
90	                """,
91	                body.name,
92	                uid,
93	            )
94	            await conn.execute(
95	                """
96	                INSERT INTO canvas_members (canvas_id, user_id)
97	                VALUES ($1, $2)
98	                """,
99	                row["id"],
100	                uid,
101	            )
102	    return CanvasSummary(
103	        id=str(row["id"]),
104	        name=row["name"],
105	        ownerId=str(row["owner_id"]),
106	        createdAt=row["created_at"].isoformat(),
107	    )
108	
109	
110	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
111	# GET /{canvas_id} — full canvas detail with shapes and members
112	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
113	
114	
115	@canvas_router.get("/{canvas_id}", response_model=None)
116	async def get_canvas(
117	    canvas_id: str,
118	    user: AuthUser = Depends(get_current_user),
119	) -> CanvasDetail | JSONResponse:
120	    pool = await get_pool()
121	    try:
122	        cid = _uuid.UUID(canvas_id)
123	    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
124	        return JSONResponse(status_code=404, content={"error": "not_found"})
125	    uid = _uuid.UUID(user["id"])
126	    async with pool.acquire() as conn:
127	        # Check canvas exists
128	        canvas_row = await conn.fetchrow(
129	            """
130	            SELECT id, name, owner_id, created_at
131	            FROM canvases
132	            WHERE id = $1
133	            """,
134	            cid,
135	        )
136	        if canvas_row is None:
137	            return JSONResponse(status_code=404, content={"error": "not_found"})
138	
139	        # Check membership
140	        membership = await conn.fetchval(
141	            """
142	            SELECT 1 FROM canvas_members
143	            WHERE canvas_id = $1 AND user_id = $2
144	            """,
145	            cid,
146	            uid,
147	        )
148	        if membership is None:
149	            return JSONResponse(status_code=403, content={"error": "not_a_member"})
150	
151	        # Load shapes
152	        shape_rows = await conn.fetch(
153	            """
154	            SELECT id, type, x, y, width, height, fill, stroke,
155	                   stroke_width, text, font_size
156	            FROM shapes
157	            WHERE canvas_id = $1
158	            ORDER BY created_at, id
159	            """,
160	            cid,
161	        )
162	
163	        # Load members
164	        member_rows = await conn.fetch(
165	            """
166	            SELECT u.id AS user_id, u.username
167	            FROM canvas_members cm
168	            JOIN users u ON u.id = cm.user_id
169	            WHERE cm.canvas_id = $1
170	            """,
171	            cid,
172	        )
173	
174	    shapes = []
175	    for r in shape_rows:
176	        s: dict = {
177	            "id": str(r["id"]),
178	            "type": r["type"],
179	            "x": r["x"],
180	            "y": r["y"],
181	            "width": r["width"],
182	            "height": r["height"],
183	            "fill": r["fill"],
184	            "stroke": r["stroke"],
185	            "strokeWidth": r["stroke_width"],
186	        }
187	        if r["text"] is not None:
188	            s["text"] = r["text"]
189	        if r["font_size"] is not None:
190	            s["fontSize"] = r["font_size"]
191	        shapes.append(s)
192	
193	    members = [
194	        CanvasMember(userId=str(r["user_id"]), username=r["username"])
195	        for r in member_rows
196	    ]
197	
198	    return CanvasDetail(
199	        id=str(canvas_row["id"]),
200	        name=canvas_row["name"],
201	        ownerId=str(canvas_row["owner_id"]),
202	        createdAt=canvas_row["created_at"].isoformat(),
203	        shapes=shapes,
204	        members=members,
205	    )
206	
207	
208	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
209	# POST /{canvas_id}/invite — invite a user by username or email
210	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
211	
212	
213	@canvas_router.post("/{canvas_id}/invite", response_model=None)
214	async def invite_to_canvas(
215	    canvas_id: str,
216	    body: InviteBody,
217	    user: AuthUser = Depends(get_current_user),
218	) -> dict | JSONResponse:
219	    pool = await get_pool()
220	    try:
221	        cid = _uuid.UUID(canvas_id)
222	    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
223	        return JSONResponse(status_code=404, content={"error": "canvas_not_found"})
224	    uid = _uuid.UUID(user["id"])
225	    async with pool.acquire() as conn:
226	        # Check canvas exists
227	        canvas_exists = await conn.fetchval(
228	            "SELECT 1 FROM canvases WHERE id = $1",
229	            cid,
230	        )
231	        if canvas_exists is None:
232	            return JSONResponse(
233	                status_code=404, content={"error": "canvas_not_found"}
234	            )
235	
236	        # Check requester is a member
237	        membership = await conn.fetchval(
238	            """
239	            SELECT 1 FROM canvas_members
240	            WHERE canvas_id = $1 AND user_id = $2
241	            """,
242	            cid,
243	            uid,
244	        )
245	        if membership is None:
246	            return JSONResponse(
247	                status_code=403, content={"error": "not_a_member"}
248	            )
249	
250	        # Look up target user by username first, then by email
251	        target = await conn.fetchrow(
252	            "SELECT id FROM users WHERE username = $1",
253	            body.identifier,
254	        )
255	        if target is None:
256	            target = await conn.fetchrow(
257	                "SELECT id FROM users WHERE email = $1",
258	                body.identifier,
259	            )
260	        if target is None:
261	            return JSONResponse(
262	                status_code=404, content={"error": "user_not_found"}
263	            )
264	
265	        # Add membership (idempotent — ignore if already a member)
266	        await conn.execute(
267	            """
268	            INSERT INTO canvas_members (canvas_id, user_id)
269	            VALUES ($1, $2)
270	            ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
271	            """,
272	            cid,
273	            target["id"],
274	        )
275	
276	    return {"ok": True}
277	
278	
279	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
280	# DELETE /{canvas_id} — delete a canvas (owner only)
281	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
282	
283	
284	@canvas_router.delete("/{canvas_id}", response_model=None)
285	async def delete_canvas(
286	    canvas_id: str,
287	    user: AuthUser = Depends(get_current_user),
288	) -> dict | JSONResponse:
289	    pool = await get_pool()
290	    try:
291	        cid = _uuid.UUID(canvas_id)
292	    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
293	        return JSONResponse(status_code=404, content={"error": "not_found"})
294	    uid = _uuid.UUID(user["id"])
295	    async with pool.acquire() as conn:
296	        canvas_row = await conn.fetchrow(
297	            "SELECT owner_id FROM canvases WHERE id = $1",
298	            cid,
299	        )
300	        if canvas_row is None:
301	            return JSONResponse(status_code=404, content={"error": "not_found"})
302	        if str(canvas_row["owner_id"]) != str(uid):
303	            return JSONResponse(status_code=403, content={"error": "not_owner"})
304	        await conn.execute("DELETE FROM canvases WHERE id = $1", cid)
305	    return {"ok": True}

38	
39	# canvas_id -> user_id -> {username, color, ws, send_lock}
40	presence: dict[str, dict[str, dict]] = defaultdict(dict)

52	def _decode_token(token: str) -> AuthUser | None:
53	    """Decode a JWT token manually (no FastAPI DI for WebSocket)."""
54	    secret = os.environ.get("JWT_SECRET", "dev-secret-do-not-use-in-prod")
55	    if not secret:
56	        return None
57	    try:
58	        payload = jwt.decode(token, secret, algorithms=["HS256"])
59	    except (jwt.ExpiredSignatureError, jwt.InvalidTokenError):
60	        return None
61	    sub = payload.get("sub")
62	    username = payload.get("username")
63	    email = payload.get("email")
64	    if not sub or not username:
65	        return None
66	    return AuthUser(id=sub, username=username, email=email or "")

69	def _row_to_shape(row: dict) -> dict:
70	    """Convert an asyncpg Record (snake_case) to a wire-format Shape (camelCase)."""
71	    shape: dict = {}
72	    for key, value in row.items():
73	        if key in ("canvas_id", "created_at"):
74	            continue  # internal, not on the wire
75	        # Omit None for optional fields (text, font_size) so the wire format
76	        # matches the NotRequired TypedDict contract.
77	        if value is None:
78	            continue
79	        wire_key = SHAPE_DB_TO_WIRE.get(key) or _EXTRA_DB_TO_WIRE.get(key, key)
80	        # UUID -> str
81	        if hasattr(value, "hex"):
82	            shape[wire_key] = str(value)
83	        else:
84	            shape[wire_key] = value
85	    return shape

88	_ALLOWED_SHAPE_COLS = frozenset({
89	    "id", "type", "x", "y", "width", "height",
90	    "fill", "stroke", "stroke_width", "text", "font_size",
91	    "opacity", "border_radius",
92	})
93	
94	
95	def _shape_to_columns(shape: dict) -> tuple[list[str], list]:
96	    """Extract DB columns and values from a wire-format Shape dict for INSERT.
97	
98	    Only whitelisted column names are accepted to prevent SQL injection
99	    through crafted key names in the shape payload.
100	    """
101	    cols: list[str] = []
102	    vals: list = []
103	    for wire_key, value in shape.items():
104	        db_key = SHAPE_WIRE_TO_DB.get(wire_key) or _EXTRA_WIRE_TO_DB.get(wire_key, wire_key)
105	        if db_key not in _ALLOWED_SHAPE_COLS:
106	            continue
107	        # asyncpg requires uuid.UUID for UUID columns
108	        if db_key == "id" and isinstance(value, str):
109	            value = _uuid.UUID(value)
110	        cols.append(db_key)
111	        vals.append(value)
112	    return cols, vals

115	async def _safe_send(ws: WebSocket, data: str, send_lock: asyncio.Lock) -> None:
116	    """Send text through a WebSocket, serialised per-connection to avoid frame interleaving."""
117	    try:
118	        async with send_lock:
119	            await ws.send_text(data)
120	    except Exception:
121	        pass  # connection already closed; disconnect handler will clean up
122	
123	
124	async def _broadcast(canvas_id: str, message: dict, *, exclude_user: str | None = None) -> None:
125	    """Broadcast a JSON message to all clients on a canvas.
126	
127	    Uses asyncio.gather with return_exceptions=True so one slow/dead
128	    client never blocks the others (CLAUDE.md gotcha #2).
129	    """
130	    payload = json.dumps(message)
131	    targets = []
132	    for uid, info in presence.get(canvas_id, {}).items():
133	        if uid == exclude_user:
134	            continue
135	        targets.append(_safe_send(info["ws"], payload, info["send_lock"]))
136	    if targets:
137	        await asyncio.gather(*targets, return_exceptions=True)
138	
139	
140	async def _broadcast_all(canvas_id: str, message: dict) -> None:
141	    """Broadcast to ALL clients on a canvas, including the sender."""
142	    payload = json.dumps(message)
143	    targets = []
144	    for _uid, info in presence.get(canvas_id, {}).items():
145	        targets.append(_safe_send(info["ws"], payload, info["send_lock"]))
146	    if targets:
147	        await asyncio.gather(*targets, return_exceptions=True)

150	# ── Ping/Pong heartbeat ─────────────────────────────────────
151	
152	PING_INTERVAL = 30  # seconds between pings
153	PONG_TIMEOUT = 10   # seconds to wait for pong reply
154	
155	
156	async def _heartbeat_loop(
157	    ws: WebSocket,
158	    send_lock: asyncio.Lock,
159	    pong_event: asyncio.Event,
160	    cancel_event: asyncio.Event,
161	) -> None:
162	    """Send periodic pings and close the connection if pong is not received in time."""
163	    try:
164	        while not cancel_event.is_set():
165	            # Wait PING_INTERVAL seconds or until cancelled
166	            try:
167	                await asyncio.wait_for(cancel_event.wait(), timeout=PING_INTERVAL)
168	                return  # cancel_event was set
169	            except asyncio.TimeoutError:
170	                pass  # time to ping
171	
172	            # Send ping
173	            pong_event.clear()
174	            await _safe_send(ws, json.dumps({"type": "ping"}), send_lock)
175	
176	            # Wait for pong
177	            try:
178	                await asyncio.wait_for(pong_event.wait(), timeout=PONG_TIMEOUT)
179	            except asyncio.TimeoutError:
180	                # No pong received — close the connection
181	                try:
182	                    await ws.close(code=1000, reason="pong timeout")
183	                except Exception:
184	                    pass
185	                return
186	    except asyncio.CancelledError:
187	        pass

191	
192	
193	async def _persist_add(conn, canvas_id: str, shape: dict) -> bool:
194	    """INSERT a new shape. Returns True if inserted, False if id already existed."""
195	    cols, vals = _shape_to_columns(shape)
196	    # Ensure canvas_id is included (as UUID for asyncpg)
197	    if "canvas_id" not in cols:
198	        cols.append("canvas_id")
199	        vals.append(_uuid.UUID(canvas_id) if isinstance(canvas_id, str) else canvas_id)
200	    placeholders = ", ".join(f"${i+1}" for i in range(len(cols)))
201	    col_names = ", ".join(cols)
202	    result = await conn.execute(
203	        f"INSERT INTO shapes ({col_names}) VALUES ({placeholders}) ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING",
204	        *vals,
205	    )
206	    # asyncpg returns 'INSERT 0 1' or 'INSERT 0 0'
207	    return result.endswith("1")
208

210	_ALLOWED_UPDATE_COLS = _ALLOWED_SHAPE_COLS - {"id", "type"}
211	
212	
213	async def _persist_update(conn, canvas_id: str, shape_id: str, props: dict) -> bool:
214	    """UPDATE only the columns in props, scoped to this canvas.
215	
216	    Returns True if a row was modified.
217	    """
218	    if not props:
219	        return False
220	    set_parts: list[str] = []
221	    vals: list = []
222	    idx = 1
223	    for wire_key, value in props.items():
224	        db_key = SHAPE_WIRE_TO_DB.get(wire_key) or _EXTRA_WIRE_TO_DB.get(wire_key, wire_key)
225	        if db_key not in _ALLOWED_UPDATE_COLS:
226	            continue
227	        set_parts.append(f"{db_key} = ${idx}")
228	        vals.append(value)
229	        idx += 1
230	    if not set_parts:
231	        return False
232	    vals.append(_uuid.UUID(shape_id) if isinstance(shape_id, str) else shape_id)
233	    vals.append(_uuid.UUID(canvas_id) if isinstance(canvas_id, str) else canvas_id)
234	    sql = f"UPDATE shapes SET {', '.join(set_parts)} WHERE id = ${idx} AND canvas_id = ${idx + 1}"
235	    result = await conn.execute(sql, *vals)
236	    # asyncpg returns 'UPDATE N'
237	    return not result.endswith("0")
238	
239	
240	async def _persist_delete(conn, canvas_id: str, shape_id: str) -> bool:
241	    """DELETE a shape, scoped to this canvas. Returns True if a row was removed."""
242	    result = await conn.execute(
243	        "DELETE FROM shapes WHERE id = $1 AND canvas_id = $2",
244	        _uuid.UUID(shape_id) if isinstance(shape_id, str) else shape_id,
245	        _uuid.UUID(canvas_id) if isinstance(canvas_id, str) else canvas_id,
246	    )
247	    return not result.endswith("0")

250	# ── Main endpoint ────────────────────────────────────────────
251	
252	
253	async def websocket_endpoint(ws: WebSocket) -> None:
254	    """WebSocket endpoint for a canvas session.
255	
256	    Connection: WS /ws?canvasId=<uuid>&token=<jwt>
257	    """
258	    # ── Auth before accept ───────────────────────────────────
259	    canvas_id = ws.query_params.get("canvasId", "")
260	    token = ws.query_params.get("token", "")
261	
262	    # Accept first to avoid Starlette/uvicorn issues with close-before-accept
263	    await ws.accept()
264	
265	    if not canvas_id or not token:
266	        await ws.close(code=4001, reason="missing canvasId or token")
267	        return
268	
269	    user = _decode_token(token)
270	    if user is None:
271	        await ws.close(code=4001, reason="auth failure")
272	        return
273	
274	    user_id: str = user["id"]
275	    username: str = user["username"]
276	
277	    # Verify canvas membership
278	    pool = await get_pool()
279	    try:
280	        cid = _uuid.UUID(canvas_id)
281	        uid = _uuid.UUID(user_id)
282	    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
283	        await ws.close(code=4001, reason="invalid canvasId or userId")
284	        return
285	
286	    async with pool.acquire(timeout=5) as conn:
287	        row = await conn.fetchrow(
288	            "SELECT 1 FROM canvas_members WHERE canvas_id = $1 AND user_id = $2",
289	            cid,
290	            uid,
291	        )
292	    if row is None:
293	        await ws.close(code=4003, reason="not a canvas member")
294	        return
295	
296	    # Per-connection send lock to prevent frame interleaving (CLAUDE.md gotcha #5)
297	    send_lock = asyncio.Lock()
298	
299	    # Heartbeat events
300	    pong_event = asyncio.Event()
301	    cancel_heartbeat = asyncio.Event()
302	
303	    # ── Presence ─────────────────────────────────────────────
304	    canvas_presence = presence[canvas_id]
305	    color_index = len(canvas_presence) % len(CURSOR_PALETTE)
306	    color = CURSOR_PALETTE[color_index]
307	
308	    me: PresenceUser = PresenceUser(
309	        userId=user_id,
310	        username=username,
311	        color=color,
312	    )
313	    canvas_presence[user_id] = {
314	        "username": username,
315	        "color": color,
316	        "ws": ws,
317	        "send_lock": send_lock,
318	    }
319	
320	    heartbeat_task = None
321	
322	    try:
323	        # ── Send init ────────────────────────────────────────
324	        async with pool.acquire(timeout=5) as conn:
325	            rows = await conn.fetch(
326	                "SELECT * FROM shapes WHERE canvas_id = $1 ORDER BY created_at, id",
327	                cid,
328	            )
329	        shapes = [_row_to_shape(dict(r)) for r in rows]
330	        users = [
331	            PresenceUser(userId=uid, username=info["username"], color=info["color"])
332	            for uid, info in canvas_presence.items()
333	        ]
334	        current_seq = seq_counters[canvas_id]
335	
336	        init_msg = json.dumps({
337	            "type": "init",
338	            "shapes": shapes,
339	            "users": users,
340	            "seq": current_seq,
341	        })
342	        async with send_lock:
343	            await ws.send_text(init_msg)
344	
345	        # Broadcast join to others
346	        await _broadcast(canvas_id, {"type": "join", "user": dict(me)}, exclude_user=user_id)
347	
348	        # Start heartbeat task
349	        heartbeat_task = asyncio.create_task(
350	            _heartbeat_loop(ws, send_lock, pong_event, cancel_heartbeat)
351	        )
352	
353	        # ── Message loop ─────────────────────────────────────
354	        while True:
355	            raw = await ws.receive_text()
356	            try:
357	                msg = json.loads(raw)
358	            except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError):
359	                continue  # ignore malformed messages
360	
361	            msg_type = msg.get("type")
362	
363	            if msg_type == "pong":
364	                pong_event.set()
365	                continue
366	
367	            if msg_type == "op":
368	                op = msg.get("op")
369	                op_id = msg.get("opId", "")
370	                if not op or not isinstance(op, dict):
371	                    continue
372	
373	                kind = op.get("kind")
374	                lock = canvas_locks[canvas_id]
375	
376	                # Acquire per-canvas lock, persist, assign seq, then
377	                # release before broadcast (contract steps 2-7).
378	                modified = False
379	                seq = 0
380	                async with lock:
381	                    async with pool.acquire(timeout=5) as conn:
382	                        if kind == "add":
383	                            shape = op.get("shape")
384	                            if shape and isinstance(shape, dict) and "id" in shape:
385	                                modified = await _persist_add(conn, canvas_id, shape)
386	                        elif kind == "update":
387	                            shape_id = op.get("shapeId")
388	                            props = op.get("props")
389	                            if shape_id and props and isinstance(props, dict):
390	                                modified = await _persist_update(conn, canvas_id, shape_id, props)
391	                        elif kind == "delete":
392	                            shape_id = op.get("shapeId")
393	                            if shape_id:
394	                                modified = await _persist_delete(conn, canvas_id, shape_id)
395	                    if modified:
396	                        seq_counters[canvas_id] += 1
397	                        seq = seq_counters[canvas_id]
398	                # Lock released — now broadcast outside the lock
399	                if modified:
400	                    await _broadcast_all(canvas_id, {
401	                        "type": "op",
402	                        "op": op,
403	                        "userId": user_id,
404	                        "seq": seq,
405	                        "opId": op_id,
406	                    })
407	                # If not modified (no-op): no broadcast, no seq increment
408	
409	            elif msg_type == "cursor":
410	                x = msg.get("x")
411	                y = msg.get("y")
412	                if x is not None and y is not None:
413	                    await _broadcast(canvas_id, {
414	                        "type": "cursor",
415	                        "userId": user_id,
416	                        "username": username,
417	                        "x": x,
418	                        "y": y,
419	                    }, exclude_user=user_id)
420	
421	    except WebSocketDisconnect:
422	        pass
423	    except Exception as exc:
424	        # Send error before disconnecting so client knows what happened
425	        try:
426	            async with send_lock:
427	                await ws.send_text(json.dumps({
428	                    "type": "error",
429	                    "message": "internal server error",
430	                }))
431	        except Exception:
432	            pass
433	    finally:
434	        # ── Cleanup ──────────────────────────────────────────
435	        cancel_heartbeat.set()
436	        if heartbeat_task is not None:
437	            try:
438	                heartbeat_task.cancel()
439	                await heartbeat_task
440	            except Exception:
441	                pass
442	
443	        # Only remove presence if this socket is still the registered one.
444	        # A second tab from the same user may have already replaced us.
445	        info = canvas_presence.get(user_id)
446	        if info and info["ws"] is ws:
447	            canvas_presence.pop(user_id, None)
448	            # Broadcast leave only if we were the active connection
449	            await _broadcast(canvas_id, {"type": "leave", "userId": user_id})
450	
451	        # Do NOT delete canvas_locks/seq_counters — avoids race on
452	        # simultaneous disconnect and prevents seq reset mid-session.
453

content.text: Now add new Key Design Decision sections for the 4 new features. I'll add them after section 7l.

input.new_string:
### 7m. Shape Opacity (Per-Shape, Toolbar Slider)

Shapes have an `opacity` property (0.0-1.0, default 1) persisted in the `shapes.opacity` DB column. The Toolbar exposes a range slider (0-100%) that updates the currently selected shape via a normal `update` op. On shape selection, `CanvasPage` reads the shape's opacity and syncs it to the `opacity` useState. New shapes inherit the current toolbar opacity if != 1. Rendering uses `ctx.globalAlpha` in `drawShape()` before any fill/stroke calls, restored via `ctx.save()`/`ctx.restore()`.

### 7n. Border Radius (Rounded Rectangles)

Rectangles support a `borderRadius` property (px, default 0) persisted in `shapes.border_radius`. When `borderRadius > 0`, `drawShape()` uses `ctx.roundRect()` instead of `fillRect()`/`strokeRect()`. The column mapping uses `_EXTRA_WIRE_TO_DB`/`_EXTRA_DB_TO_WIRE` in `ws.py` since the mapping is not in the shared `types.py` SHAPE maps. No UI control for setting borderRadius yet -- it can be set programmatically or by future toolbar extension.

### 7o. Text Hit-Testing via measureText

Text shapes previously had unreliable hit-testing because their `width`/`height` were set at creation time and might not match the actual rendered size. Now `drawShape()` calls `ctx.measureText()` during rendering to compute the actual text width and updates `shape.width` and `shape.height` in place (`fontSize * 1.2` for line height). This ensures `isPointInShape()` uses accurate bounds for text selection.

### 7p. Canvas Deletion (Owner-Only)

Canvas owners can delete their canvases via a new `DELETE /api/canvases/{id}` endpoint. The server checks `canvases.owner_id` against the JWT user and returns 403 `not_owner` if they differ. On success, PostgreSQL's `ON DELETE CASCADE` on `shapes.canvas_id` and `canvas_members.canvas_id` removes all associated rows. The client shows a delete button (X icon) on canvas cards in `CanvasList`, visible on hover, with a `confirm()` dialog before calling `api.deleteCanvas()`. Non-owners see a "Only the canvas owner can delete it" error.

---

content.content:
690	### 8f. What's NOT Implemented
691	
692	| Feature | Status |
693	|---------|--------|
694	| **WebSocket reconnection** | Implemented. Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff (up to 10 attempts, max 30s delay with jitter). Manual retry button after exhaustion. See 7j. |
695	| **Ping/pong heartbeat** | Implemented. Server pings every 30s, closes connection if no pong within 10s. See 7k. |
696	| **Cursor interpolation (lerp)** | Implemented. rAF loop interpolates at factor 0.15 per frame; opacity fades to 0.3 after 5s idle. See 7l. |
697	| **Reconnect state recovery** | No incremental sync. Reconnect triggers a fresh `init` from the server, resetting shapes, presence, undo/redo stacks, and pending ops. |
698	| **Rate limiting** | No connection or message rate limits on WS or HTTP. |
699	| **Input validation** | Minimal. WS messages with missing fields are silently ignored, but no schema validation. |
700	| **Text editing in-place** | Text creation uses `window.prompt()`. No in-canvas text editing overlay. |
701	| **Z-order manipulation** | Shapes are z-ordered by creation time (array index). Selected shape is drawn last for visual lift but array order is not mutated. No bring-to-front/send-to-back user action. |
702	| **Multi-select** | Only single shape selection. No box select or Shift+click. |
703	| **Pending ops divergence recovery** | If server ignores an op (no-op), the pendingOps set grows unboundedly until reconnect. |
704	| **seq counter persistence** | seq resets on server restart. Clients self-correct on reconnect via fresh `init`. |
705	| **Connection pool tuning for WS** | Pool is min=2, max=20. Each WS op holds a connection briefly. Could exhaust under load. |
706	| **Structured logging** | No logging anywhere in the backend. Errors are silently caught in WS handler. |
707

614	## 8. Known Issues / Integration Notes
615	
616	### 8a. Error Envelope Handler in main.py
617	
618	```python
619	@app.exception_handler(HTTPException)
620	async def http_exception_handler(request, exc):
621	    detail = exc.detail if isinstance(exc.detail, str) else "error"
622	    return JSONResponse(status_code=exc.status_code, content={"error": detail})
623	```
624	
625	This overrides FastAPI's default exception handler so ALL HTTPExceptions return `{"error": "<code>"}` instead of `{"detail": "<message>"}`. This is critical for the client's `ApiError` class, which reads `err.error` from the response body. Without this handler, auth failures would return `{"detail": "unauthorized"}` and the client would see `error: "unknown"`.
626	
627	Note that `auth.py` and `canvas.py` return `JSONResponse` directly for error cases (not raising HTTPException), so this handler only catches errors from `deps.py get_current_user()` and any unhandled FastAPI validation errors.
628	
629	### 8b. UUID String-to-Object Conversion in ws.py
630	
631	asyncpg requires `uuid.UUID` objects for UUID columns, not strings. The WebSocket handler receives shape IDs as strings from JSON and must convert them:
632	
633	```python
634	if db_key == "id" and isinstance(value, str):
635	    value = _uuid.UUID(value)
636	```
637	
638	This conversion happens in `_shape_to_columns()` for inserts, and explicitly in `_persist_update()` and `_persist_delete()` for WHERE clauses. Canvas ID and user ID are similarly converted at the top of `websocket_endpoint()`. Failure to convert would cause asyncpg `DataError`.
639	
640	### 8c. StrictMode Double-Mount Guard
641	
642	```typescript
643	// CanvasPage.tsx
644	const mountedRef = useRef(false);
645	
646	useEffect(() => {
647	    if (mountedRef.current) return;  // skip second mount in StrictMode
648	    mountedRef.current = true;
649	    // ... connect WS, load canvas ...
650	    return () => {
651	        mountedRef.current = false;
652	        ws.close();
653	    };
654	}, [canvasId]);
655	```
656	
657	React 19 StrictMode in development double-fires effects (mount -> unmount -> remount). Without this guard, two WebSocket connections would open simultaneously, causing duplicate messages, presence entries, and duplicate reconnection timers. The `mountedRef` flag ensures only the first mount actually connects. The cleanup function sets `mountedRef = false`, clears `shouldReconnectRef`, cancels any pending reconnect timer, and closes the WebSocket.
658	
659	### 8d. HTTP/WS Init Race Condition Fix

696	| **Cursor interpolation (lerp)** | Implemented. rAF loop interpolates at factor 0.15 per frame; opacity fades to 0.3 after 5s idle. See 7l. |
697	| **Shape opacity** | Implemented. Per-shape opacity (0-1) with toolbar slider, persisted in DB. See 7m. |
698	| **Border radius** | Implemented (backend + renderer). No toolbar UI yet. See 7n. |
699	| **Text hit-testing** | Fixed. measureText computes accurate bounds at render time. See 7o. |
700	| **Canvas deletion** | Implemented. Owner-only DELETE endpoint, client delete button on cards. See 7p. |
701	| **Reconnect state recovery** | No incremental sync. Reconnect triggers a fresh `init` from the server, resetting shapes, presence, undo/redo stacks, and pending ops. |
702	| **Rate limiting** | No connection or message rate limits on WS or HTTP. |
703	| **Input validation** | Minimal. WS messages with missing fields are silently ignored, but no schema validation. |
704	| **Text editing in-place** | Text creation uses `window.prompt()`. No in-canvas text editing overlay. |
705	| **Z-order manipulation** | Shapes are z-ordered by creation time (array index). Selected shape is drawn last for visual lift but array order is not mutated. No bring-to-front/send-to-back user action. |
706	| **Multi-select** | Only single shape selection. No box select or Shift+click. |
707	| **Pending ops divergence recovery** | If server ignores an op (no-op), the pendingOps set grows unboundedly until reconnect. |
708	| **seq counter persistence** | seq resets on server restart. Clients self-correct on reconnect via fresh `init`. |
709	| **Connection pool tuning for WS** | Pool is min=2, max=20. Each WS op holds a connection briefly. Could exhaust under load. |
